hi bro, I’d like to gently clarify a small misunderstanding regarding waterheart .

waterheart’s decision not to pull xu is not because he thinks xu is weak or unsuitable for poison teams. in fact, his view is quite the opposite—he fully recognizes that xu is significantly stronger than lily in overall performance, including both survivability and damage output, and that xu is a major upgrade for poison-based compositions.

the reason he chose not to pull xu is simply personal preference. he really enjoys playing lily and prefers to keep using her, and since xu would effectively replace lily’s role in the team, he decided to stick with his original choice.

there may have been some unintentional misinterpretation when discussing his decision. waterheart does not think xu is a weak character. he respects xu’s strength, but is choosing to prioritize his attachment to lily.

just wanted to clear this up so there’s no confusion. thanks for understanding!

lily's healing is indeed strong, but it's severely limited under the astral reign rules. 

lily needs to constantly activate her over exaltation to prepare resources for damage output, but she can't use strike to protect before the poison stacks up. this constant activation of over exaltation leads to a rapid acceleration of her scar development in the early game, making her survivability very weak in the late game.

xu is less affected by scar. her main survival methods are reducing power and damage. her over exaltation can also provide a large number of crimson furnace, but this amount is insufficient to trigger scar.

embeds.title: Morimens: Kathigu-Ra Overview #morimens #animehorrorgame #lovecraft...
embeds.description:
A series that provides a 60 second overview of the Awakeners in Morimens!
Anime + Lovecraftian cosmos makes for a memorable gameplay experience. The characters are beautifully rendered and the story is far darker than I was anticipating. This is a very fun and intriguing card battler game.

I think that with regards to gacha games, Morimens is p friendly as a F2P because a lot of mechanisms allow for you to move forward in the game without spending extra cash.

Does extra cash help?   Definitely.  

Is it _the most F2P friendly game?_  Shit I haven't tried that many.

But I think that we shouldn't necessarily say "it's F2P unfriendly", esp if you're comping it to a paid game.  If you wanted to avoid a gacha game, you would.  It's not a comp that exists because it's senseless-- it's as absurd as shitting on vegetarian food when you have _zero_ desire, experience, or stake in eating vegetarian.

F2P Friendly is a descriptor that gets applied to the game based on how hard it is to play the content without spending money. The degree to which you want it to apply can vary, so generally you wouldn't set a hard limit on what constitutes friendly since it tends to be more of a spectrum
